Summary: Mid-year report card for 3rd grader Emma Miller dated February 14, 2026, detailing strong performance in academics and social skills with three excused absences.
results._user:
Next document:

---
FOREST GROVE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL
Principal's Office | 15 Forest Road | Portland, OR 97201

Mid-Year Report Card — School Year 2025/26

Student: Emma Miller
Grade: 3B
Teacher: Dr. Schneider
Date: 02/14/2026

English Language Arts:
Emma reads fluently and with good expression. She understands texts quickly and can summarize their content accurately. Her essays are creative and well-structured. Spelling is solid, with occasional errors on double consonants. She participates regularly in class discussions and contributes her own ideas.

Mathematics:
Emma has a strong grasp of numbers up to 1,000. She completes addition and subtraction problems quickly and accurately. She shows good understanding of written multiplication, though division sometimes takes her a bit longer. She solves word problems methodically and can explain her reasoning. She identifies and names geometric shapes reliably.

Science:
Emma shows strong interest in natural science topics. For the "Our Forest" project she prepared an excellent presentation on mushrooms. She asks thoughtful questions and works carefully during experiments.

Art:
Emma creates with imagination and persistence. Her color compositions are mature for her age. She enjoys trying new techniques and helps classmates with their projects.

Physical Education:
Emma is physically coordinated and shows good effort. In team sports she is fair and supports weaker teammates. In swimming class she earned her Bronze swimming badge.

Social Skills:
Emma is friendly, helpful, and respectful toward classmates and teachers. She resolves conflicts calmly and gladly takes on classroom responsibilities.

Work Habits:
Emma works independently and reliably. Her notebooks are neatly kept. She completes homework conscientiously. She concentrates well and manages her time effectively.

Days absent: 3 (excused)

Portland, 02/14/2026

_________________________          _________________________
Dr. Schneider                      Mr. Brown
Classroom Teacher                  Principal
---

DR. KARLA HOFFMAN, MD
Family Medicine
3 Linden Plaza | Portland, OR 97205
Tel: 503-555-7890 | Fax: 503-555-7891

WELL-CHILD VISIT REPORT

Patient: Max Miller, DOB 07/15/2019
Date of examination: 03/03/2026

Reason for visit:
5-year well-child checkup

Findings:

Physical Development:
  Height: 46.5 in (75th percentile)
  Weight: 47.0 lbs (65th percentile)
  BMI: 15.3 (normal range)
  General condition good, age-appropriate nutrition

Vision:
  Vision screening passed both eyes (Lea Symbols)
  No strabismus, stereopsis present

Hearing:
  Audiometry screening unremarkable both ears

Motor Skills:
  Gross motor: Hops on one foot confidently, catches ball, rides bicycle without training wheels
  Fine motor: Draws person with 6+ details, cuts out shapes, ties simple knots
  Age-appropriate development

Speech & Language:
  Speaks in complete, grammatically correct sentences
  Counts to 20, knows colors and shapes
  Tells coherent stories
  Mild difficulty with /sh/ and /ch/ sounds — speech therapy evaluation recommended

Social-Emotional Development:
  Age-appropriate. Plays cooperatively, can wait turns, understands simple rules.
  Parents report occasional tantrums during transitions (leaving playground, etc.) — within normal range for age.

Immunizations:
  DTaP/IPV booster administered (Kinrix)
  Next due: flu vaccine in fall 2026

Recommendations:
  1. Schedule speech therapy appointment for /sh/ and /ch/ sounds
  2. Annual dental checkup (last visit >12 months ago)
  3. Next well-child visit: age 7-8 (pre-kindergarten)

School readiness from medical perspective: likely ready.
School district screening still pending.

Portland, 03/03/2026

_________________________
Dr. Karla Hoffman, MD

PACIFIC NORTHWEST INSURANCE GROUP

Notice of Policy Adjustment

Dear Mr. Miller,

Due to the annual adjustment of your insurance policy to the current homeowner's insurance index, we are notifying you of the following changes, effective 04/01/2026:

Previous coverage amount:       $285,000.00
New coverage amount:            $293,550.00 (increase 3.0%)

Previous annual premium:          $1,812.00
New annual premium:               $1,866.36 (increase 3.0%)
Monthly premium:                    $155.53

Your coverage summary:
  - Dwelling and personal property against fire, theft, water damage, windstorm/hail
  - Bicycle theft up to $2,000 (including away from home)
  - Power surge damage from lightning
  - Hotel costs if uninhabitable up to $100/day, max 30 days
  - Deductible: $500 per claim
  - Guaranteed replacement cost with accurate square footage

Current square footage on file: 1,022 sq ft

Important: If your home's square footage has changed (e.g., addition, finished basement), please notify us within 30 days to ensure your coverage remains adequate.

You have the right to object to this adjustment within one month of receiving this notice. In that case, your policy will terminate at the next renewal date.

Questions? Contact us:

Email: service@pnw-insurance.example.com
Online: www.my-pnw-insurance.example.com

S. Lechner
Property Insurance Department
Pacific Northwest Insurance Group

- Revised policy terms (2026 edition)

results._response:
Category: Legal
Tags: Lease Agreement, Residential Rental, Security Deposit, Rent Terms, Oregon
Summary: Residential lease between Cascade Property Management LLC and tenants Thomas and Sarah Miller for 742 Maple Street, Unit 2A, effective 05/01/2021 with a monthly rent of $1,970.00 and security deposit of $1,970.00.
results._user:
Process this multi-page document:

---
RESIDENTIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

Between
  Cascade Property Management LLC
  represented by Stefan Huber, Managing Partner
  500 Congress Center Blvd, Suite 400, Portland, OR 97204
  Tax ID: 93-1234567
  — hereinafter "Landlord" —

and
  Thomas Miller and Sarah Miller
  currently residing at: 12 Birch Lane, Portland, OR 97225
  — hereinafter "Tenant" —

The following lease agreement is entered into:

SECTION 1: PREMISES

The Landlord leases to the Tenant the following dwelling:
  Address: 742 Maple Street, Unit 2A, Portland, OR 97201
  Living area: approximately 1,022 sq ft
  Rooms: 4 (3 bedrooms, kitchen, bathroom, hallway, storage closet)
  Year built: 1962, last renovation: 2019
  Energy rating: 87 kBtu/sq ft/yr, Class C
  Heating: Central gas furnace

Included with the unit:
  - Storage unit #7 in basement (approx. 85 sq ft)
  - 1 parking space in underground garage (#TG-14)
  - Shared use: laundry room, drying room, bicycle storage, garden

Not included:
  - Attic (restricted access)
  - Community sauna in basement (separate agreement required)

SECTION 2: LEASE TERM

The lease begins on 05/01/2021 and runs for an indefinite period.
Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice before the end of the month.
After 12 months of tenancy, the Landlord's notice period extends to 60 days.
After 24 months, the Landlord's notice period extends to 90 days.
Oregon state law (ORS 90.427) governs all termination procedures.

SECTION 3: RENT AND UTILITIES

3.1 Base Rent:
  Dwelling:     $1,850.00/month
  Parking:        $120.00/month
  Total:        $1,970.00/month

3.2 Utilities included in rent:
  Water/sewer, garbage collection, recycling

3.3 Utilities paid by Tenant:
  Electricity, gas (heating), internet, renter's insurance

3.4 Estimated monthly utility costs (for reference only):
  Gas (heating/hot water):   $145.00/month (winter avg)
  Electricity:                $85.00/month
  Internet:                   $65.00/month

3.5 Total estimated monthly cost: $2,265.00/month

3.6 Rent is due on the 1st of each month. A grace period of 4 days
    is provided. Late fee of $50.00 applies after the 5th.
    Payment by check or electronic transfer to:
    Cascade Property Management LLC
    Account ending in: ****3456
    Reference: Rent 742Maple/2A - Miller

SECTION 4: SECURITY DEPOSIT

4.1 The Tenant shall pay a security deposit equal to one month's rent:
    $1,970.00

4.2 The deposit shall be held in a trust account per Oregon law (ORS 90.300).

4.3 The deposit shall be returned within 31 days of lease termination,
    less any lawful deductions for:
    - Unpaid rent or utilities
    - Damage beyond normal wear and tear
    - Cleaning costs if unit is not left in reasonable condition

4.4 An itemized statement of any deductions shall accompany
    the deposit return.

SECTION 5: CONDITION OF PREMISES

5.1 The Tenant has inspected the unit and accepts it in its
    current condition, subject to the following noted defects:

5.2 Move-in condition report (attached as Exhibit C):
    - Bedroom 2: Scratch on hardwood floor near window (approx. 12x8 in)
    - Bathroom: Caulking around bathtub discolored (Landlord will replace
      within 4 weeks of move-in)
    - Kitchen: Dishwasher connection leaking (will be repaired before move-in)

5.3 A detailed move-in checklist with photographs is attached as Exhibit C.

SECTION 6: MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS

6.1 The Landlord is responsible for:
    - Structural repairs and maintenance
    - Plumbing, electrical, and HVAC systems
    - Appliance repair/replacement (those provided by Landlord)
    - Common area maintenance
    - Pest control

6.2 The Tenant is responsible for:
    - Keeping the unit clean and sanitary
    - Minor maintenance (light bulbs, HVAC filters, smoke detector batteries)
    - Reporting maintenance issues promptly in writing
    - Damage caused by Tenant, household members, or guests

6.3 Emergency maintenance (burst pipe, no heat in winter, electrical hazard)
    should be reported immediately to: 503-555-9111 (24-hour line)

SECTION 7: HOUSE RULES

The attached house rules (Exhibit B) are part of this agreement.
Key provisions:
  - Quiet hours: 10:00 PM - 7:00 AM daily
  - No charcoal or gas grills on balconies. Electric grills permitted.
  - Smoking: Permitted inside unit only, not in common areas or balcony
  - Pets: Small caged animals (fish, hamsters) allowed. Dogs and cats
    require prior written approval from Landlord. Pet deposit of $500 applies.
  - Musical instruments: Maximum 2 hours daily, not during quiet hours
  - Parking: One assigned space only. No vehicle repairs in garage.
  - Storage: No flammable materials in storage unit or common areas.

SECTION 8: GENERAL PROVISIONS

8.1 Amendments to this agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties.
8.2 If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions
    shall remain in effect.
8.3 This agreement is governed by Oregon Residential Landlord and Tenant Act.
8.4 Two copies of this agreement are prepared, one for each party.

Exhibits:
  A. Floor plan of the unit
  B. House rules
  C. Move-in condition report with photographs
  D. Lead-based paint disclosure (pre-1978 building)
  E. Mold information disclosure
  F. Security deposit receipt

Portland, OR, 04/15/2021

_________________________          _________________________
Stefan Huber                       Thomas Miller
Cascade Property Management        Tenant
LLC, Managing Partner

                                   _________________________
                                   Sarah Miller
                                   Tenant
---
